scope.goal: Map the shared helper functions reached by the locale constructor family and fold the reusable decoder gap exposed by those helpers back into Reverser.
scope.relationship_to_previous_milestone: This follows the locale constructor caller graph downward into the helper functions shared by 0x1401868F0, 0x14047A480, 0x14047AA60, and 0x1407A8D80.

locale_name_field_initializer_0x1407b609.role: Shared locale-name field initializer/parser for the constructor family.
locale_name_field_initializer_0x1407b609.confidence: high for call graph and field writes; medium for exact standard-library type naming.
observed_behavior: Saves incoming RCX as the destination object and incoming RDX as an optional locale/name pointer.
Calls 0x1407D3610 with ECX=0 and EDX=0, falls back to rdata anchor 0x140B5DCB8 when the result is null, then writes the result through 0x1407B5E14 into [object+0x48].
If the original RDX argument is non-null, calls 0x1407D3610 with ECX=0 and RDX=argument.
Falls back to literal/anchor 0x140B9CD84 when the second normalized pointer is null, then tail-jumps to 0x1407B5E14 to write [object+0x58].

constant_readback.0x140B5DCB8: Begins with zero bytes before nearby LUMINANCE_ADAPTED text; treated as an empty/default rdata anchor pending wider string-table mapping.
constant_readback.0x140B9CD84: ASCII '*' followed by adjacent unrelated strings; likely wildcard/default marker for the second field.

locale_name_finalizer_0x1407b6104.confidence: high for pairing and direct caller set; medium for exact side effect of 0x1407D3610.
observed_behavior: If non-null, calls 0x1407D3610 with ECX=0 and RDX=[RCX+0x48].
Ignores the return value and returns.

bad_locale_exception_wrappers.role: Exception/report wrapper cluster used by the bad-locale constructor tails and neighboring standard exception paths.
bad_locale_exception_wrappers.confidence: high for throw/report wrapper structure and literals; medium for exact exception class names beyond literal evidence.

observed_behavior: Builds a stack-local object through 0x1407B5560 using the incoming message pointer.

literal_evidence: 0x140805148 contains 'Unknown exception' and is returned by helper 0x1407B57E8 when [RCX+0x08] is null.
0x140805178 contains 'bad allocation' in the neighboring descriptor row.
0x1408051A0 contains 'bad function call' and is returned by helper 0x1407B57E0.
The previously mapped constructor tails pass 'bad locale name' at 0x140B6A720 into 0x1407B57BC.

locale_object_lifetime_island_0x1407b5e8.role: Object lifetime and default-singleton support around the shared locale helpers.
locale_object_lifetime_island_0x1407b5e8.confidence: high for object sizes, globals, and call chains; medium for exact public type names.

members.0x1407B5E8C: Destructor-like routine for a 0x38-byte object; installs descriptor 0x140806700, calls 0x1407B601C, frees [object+0x28], installs descriptor 0x1408066E0, and optionally deletes the object when DIL bit 0 is set.
members.0x1407B5EE8: Registers an object into global singly-linked list 0x140C55918 by allocating a 0x10-byte node and storing the object pointer at node+0x08.
members.0x1407B5F28: Default singleton initializer using guard helpers 0x1407B6330/0x1407B63A8; allocates through 0x1407B6120, initializes through 0x1407B6150, writes 0x3F to [object+0x20], copies the rdata table at 0x140B7304C into [object+0x28], calls a vtable slot through pointer 0x140803060, and publishes globals 0x140C55908 and 0x140C558D8.
members.0x1407B601C: Cleanup routine that walks entries from vector [object+0x10] using count [object+0x18], dispatches cleanup through vtable slots, frees the vector, and exits through 0x1407B63A8.
members.0x1407B6120: Allocates 0x38 bytes and calls 0x1407B5D8C when allocation succeeds.

constant_readback.0x140B7304C: Wide/word-like rdata table beginning with C, D, F, G, 8, 9, A, B; exact semantic label remains pending.

guard_and_default_state_helpers_0x1407b6.role: Guard/default-state helper island used by locale object initialization and cleanup.
guard_and_default_state_helpers_0x1407b6.confidence: high for global accesses and iteration bounds; medium for concurrency or CRT-equivalent naming.

members.0x1407B62EC: Increments global dword 0x140C36004 and, on wrap to zero, iterates slot rows 0x140C559C0..0x140C55B00 in 0x28-byte steps calling 0x1407B7BA8.
members.0x1407B6330: Stores guard id EDX to [RCX]; id zero calls 0x1407D3714, and ids 1..7 index 0x140C559C0 + id*0x28 then call 0x1407B7BB8.
members.0x1407B636C: Decrements global dword 0x140C36004 with LOCK XADD and, when the result is negative, iterates 0x140C559C0..0x140C55B00 calling 0x1407B7BA0.
members.0x1407B63A8: Finalizes guard id [RCX]; id zero jumps to 0x1407D3730, and ids 1..7 index 0x140C559C0 + id*0x28 then call 0x1407B7BC0.

byte_classification_builder_0x1407b7844.role: Builds a 0x30-byte classification record used by the bool/numeric facet-style constructor path.
byte_classification_builder_0x1407b7844.confidence: high for bitset construction; medium for exact facet name.
observed_behavior: Clears the destination record at RCX.
Stores return values from 0x1407D3770 and 0x1407D3804 at offsets 0x00 and 0x04.
Calls 0x1407D37D0, checks context pointer [RAX+0x10], and stores a null-context flag at [record+0x08].
When context exists, calls 0x1407D3740 and scans 256 signed 16-bit entries.
For negative entries, sets the corresponding bit in [record+0x0C + index/8] using BTS ECX, EAX.

codepage_or_context_record_0x1407b79e0.role: Small record initializer used by the regex/pattern-adjacent locale constructor path.
codepage_or_context_record_0x1407b79e0.confidence: medium-high for field layout and ownership; medium for exact type name.
observed_behavior: Loads context through 0x1407D37D0 and copies [context+0x08] into [record+0x08].
If that pointer is non-null, duplicates or retains it through 0x1407D3E98 and stores the returned pointer at [record+0x08].

descriptor_rows.0x140805138: Exception descriptor row containing helpers 0x1407B55BC and 0x1407B57E8 plus inline 'Unknown exception' text.
descriptor_rows.0x1408058B8: Neighbor exception descriptor row with repeated 0x1400B32A0 and 0x1407B57E8 slots.
descriptor_rows.0x1408066E0: Descriptor row installed during 0x38-byte object destructor/finalizer state transitions.
descriptor_rows.0x140806700: Descriptor row installed before cleanup 0x1407B601C in the destructor-like routine.

decoder_gaps_closed: Two-byte bit-test register/memory forms 0F A3, 0F AB, 0F B3, and 0F BB now decode as BT, BTS, BTR, and BTC.
The exposed client instruction at 0x1407B78B4 now decodes as BTS ECX, EAX instead of an unknown DB fallback.
tooling_updates.why_it_matters: The bit-test family appears inside the native byte-classification builder and is likely to recur in other table/bitset-heavy client routines. Keeping it native lets later exporter passes preserve control-flow alignment and useful instruction semantics.

validation_notes.qword_warning_explanation: Expected for virtual-only .data readback at 0x140C55B00; the guard-slot range endpoint is still visible from instruction evidence.

next_targets: Map 0x1407B6150 and 0x1407B5D8C to complete the 0x38-byte locale/default object constructor lifecycle.
Trace guard-slot helpers 0x1407B7BA0, 0x1407B7BA8, 0x1407B7BB8, and 0x1407B7BC0 to name the 0x140C559C0 slot-row structure.
Follow 0x1407D3610 and the 0x1407D37xx family to connect these helpers to the underlying runtime locale/context provider.
Map 0x14047B290, 0x14047B9E0, 0x14047BAC0, and 0x140479C90 to tie the locale helper family back into numeric/facet parsing and multibyte conversion.
